lede: A look at August 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Claude Debussy and Christina Obergföll, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2012–2013 Tana River District clashes
events.desc: Ethnic clashes over grazing rights for cattle in Kenya's Tana River District result in more than 52 deaths.

events.title: Grigori Perelman
events.desc: Grigori Perelman is awarded the Fields Medal for his proof of the Poincaré conjecture in mathematics but refuses to accept the medal.

events.title: Iceland
events.desc: Iceland is the first nation in the world to recognize the independence of the Baltic states.

events.title: 1985 Manchester Airport disaster
events.desc: British Airtours Flight 28M suffers an engine fire during takeoff at Manchester Airport. The pilots abort but due to inefficient evacuation procedures 55 people are killed, mostly from smoke inhalation.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Holocaust of Kedros in Crete by German forces.

events.title: Brazil
events.desc: Brazil declares war on Germany, Japan and Italy.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: Mahatma Gandhi forms the Natal Indian Congress (NIC) in order to fight discrimination against Indian traders in Natal.

events.title: Haiti
events.desc: The Haitian slave revolution begins in Saint-Domingue, Haiti.

events.title: Charles I of England
events.desc: Charles I raises his standard in Nottingham, which marks the beginning of the English Civil War.

events.title: Battle of Bosworth Field
events.desc: The Battle of Bosworth Field occurs; King Richard III of England's death in battle marks the end of the reigning Plantagenet dynasty and the beginning of the Tudors under Henry VII.

births.name: Deng Xiaoping
births.line: Chinese soldier and politician, paramount leader and 1st Vice Premier of the People's Republic of China

births.name: Alun Michael
births.line: Welsh police commissioner and politician, inaugural First Minister of Wales

births.name: Ray Bradbury
births.line: American science fiction writer and screenwriter

deaths.text: Franz Joseph Gall, Austrian neuroanatomist and physiologist
Dagobert Sigmund von Wurmser, French-Austrian field marshal

observances.text: Christian feast day: Immaculate Heart of Mary (Roman Catholic calendar of 1960)
International Day Commemorating the Victims of Acts of Violence Based on Religion or Belief
Earliest day on which National Heroes' Day (Philippines) can fall, while August 28 is the latest; celebrated on the fourth Monday in August.
